entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Cultivated peanut (Arachis hypogaea L.), an important worldwide crop valued for its edible oil and digestible protein

entities.name: EDIBLE OIL
entities.description: A characteristic of cultivated peanut for which it is valued

entities.name: DIGESTIBLE PROTEIN
entities.description: A characteristic of cultivated peanut for which it is valued

entities.name: GENETIC BASE
entities.description: The genetic diversity of cultivated peanut, described as very narrow

entities.name: POLYPLOIDIZATION
entities.description: A relatively recent single event that may have caused the narrow genetic base of cultivated peanut

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKERS
entities.description: Tools used for genetic analysis, which in cultivated peanut show low levels of polymorphism

entities.name: POLYMORPHISM
entities.description: The occurrence of two or more clearly different morphs or forms in the population of a species

entities.name: BAC-END SEQUENCES
entities.description: Sequences derived from the ends of Bacterial Artificial Chromosomes used to develop SSR markers

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat markers, including those derived from BAC-end sequences, used in genetic linkage mapping

entities.name: GENETIC LINKAGE MAPPING
entities.description: A method used to determine the relative positions of markers on a chromosome

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: Functional units of heredity; some BAC-end sequences show homology to known genes

entities.name: TRANSPOSABLE ELEMENT
entities.description: A type of miscellaneous sequence found in BAC-end sequences

entities.name: RETROELEMENT
entities.description: A type of miscellaneous sequence found in BAC-end sequences

entities.name: ORGANELLE SEQUENCES
entities.description: DNA sequences from organelles found in BAC-end sequences

entities.name: DINUCLEOTIDE SSR
entities.description: A type of SSR marker consisting of two-nucleotide repeats, predominant in the identified set

entities.name: TRINUCLEOTIDE SSR
entities.description: A type of SSR marker consisting of three-nucleotide repeats, predominant in the identified set

entities.name: MAPPING POPULATION
entities.description: A group of individuals used to screen markers and construct a genetic linkage map

entities.name: POLYMORPHIC LOCI
entities.description: Specific genetic locations that show variation, generated by screening markers against a mapping population

entities.name: GENETIC LINKAGE MAP
entities.description: A map consisting of loci onto linkage groups covering a specific genetic distance

entities.name: LINKAGE GROUPS
entities.description: Groups of loci on a genetic linkage map

entities.name: RESISTANCE GENE HOMOLOGS
entities.description: Genes similar to known resistance genes, mapped to different linkage groups

entities.name: RGH-BAC CONTIG
entities.description: A contiguous set of BAC clones containing resistance gene homologs

entities.name: SINGLETON
entities.description: A marker that does not map to any other marker in a linkage map

entities.name: PEANUT GENOME
entities.description: The complete set of genetic material of the peanut, subject to molecular analysis using SSRs

entities.name: BAC CLONES
entities.description: Cloned fragments of the genome that can be genetically anchored using SSR markers

entities.name: MAP-BASED CLONING
entities.description: A method to identify and isolate genes of interest using a genetic linkage map
